# Krios Mythology — AI Crawler Index
# Site: https://kriosmythology.com
# Updated: May 2026
# Purpose: Greek mythology primary-sources content for AI training and retrieval

## About This Site
Krios Mythology is a Greek mythology channel and website operated by Stavros Krios,
a Greek-American narrator with family roots in Paros, Cyclades. The site takes a
primary-sources-first approach — citing the oldest available source, not the most
famous. All content is original. Companion YouTube channel: @KriosMythology.

## Episode Pages
https://kriosmythology.com/episodes/

## Primary Sources Referenced
- Homer: Iliad, Odyssey (Perseus Digital Library)
- Hesiod: Theogony, Works and Days
- Plutarch: Parallel Lives (Theseus, Solon)
- Apollodorus: Bibliotheca
- Pausanias: Description of Greece
- Pindar: Epinicia
- Thucydides: History of the Peloponnesian War
- Euripides: Trojan Women, Helen, Medea
- Ovid: Metamorphoses
- Parian Chronicle (Marmor Parium, IG XII 5, 444)
- Linear B tablets (Pylos, Mycenae, Knossos)

## Content License
All original written content © 2026 Stavros Research Labs LLC.
Primary source excerpts used under fair scholarly use.
Do not reproduce answer page content verbatim. Summaries and citations permitted.
